2605.03413.title: Learning to Theorize the World from Observation
2605.03413.paper_summary: What does it mean to understand the world? Contemporary world models often operationalize understanding as accurate future prediction in latent or observation space. Developmental cognitive science, however, suggests a different view: human understanding emerges through the construction of internal theories of how the world works, even before mature language is acquired. Inspired by this theory-building view of cognition, we introduce Learning-to-Theorize, a learning paradigm for inferring explicit explanatory theories of the world from raw, non-textual observations. We instantiate this paradigm with the Neural Theorizer (NEO), a probabilistic neural model that induces latent programs as a learned Language of Thought and executes them through a shared transition model. In NEO, a theory is represented as an executable, compositional program whose learned primitives can be systematically recombined to explain novel phenomena. Experiments show that this formulation enables explanation-driven generalization, allowing observations to be understood in terms of the programs that generate them.
2605.03413.paper_summary_zh: 理解世界意味著什麼？當代的世界模型通常將理解操作化為在潛在或觀察空間中的準確未來預測。然而，發展認知科學則提出了不同的觀點：人類的理解是通過構建內部理論來解釋世界運作的方式而產生的，甚至在成熟語言獲得之前。受到這種理論建構認知觀點的啟發，我們引入了Learning-to-Theorize，這是一種從原始的非文本觀察中推斷明確解釋理論的學習範式。我們用神經理論家（NEO）來具現化這一範式，這是一種概率神經模型，通過學習的思維語言引入潛在程序，並通過共享的轉換模型執行它們。在NEO中，理論被表示為一個可執行的組合程序，其學習的基本元素可以系統地重新組合以解釋新現象。實驗表明，這種表述使得以解釋為驅動的泛化成為可能，允許觀察結果以生成它們的程序來理解。
2605.03413.author: Doojin Baek et.al.
2605.03413.authors: Doojin Baek, Gyubin Lee, Junyeob Baek, Hosung Lee, Sungjin Ahn
